messages.content:
Select any words that are parallel to the bold word(s).
During the scavenger hunt, Dwayne Johnson and I had to gather
new photos of all our friends, traffic cones from Main Street, and
six bottles of Coke.

- new photos
- traffic cones
- six bottles of Coke

Which of the following statements is TRUE?
If there is a mistake on one of your bank accounts,
there is nothing you can do about it.
If there is a mistake on one of your bank accounts,
you should wait 30 days as these issues tend to
resolve themselves.
If there is a mistake on your bank account, you
should contact your financial institution
immediately.
None of the above.
If there is a mistake on your bank account, you should contact your financial institution immediately.

Which type of bank account typically offers the least (if
any) interest?
Money market account
Savings account
Checking account
Certificate of deposit
Checking account typically offers the least interest (if any).

A stock is
A share of ownership in a company.
A type of debt investment that acts like a loan.
A type of savings account that pays interest based
on current interest rates in the money market.
A type of investment that invests in a mix of
different types of investments.
A stock is a share of ownership in a company.

Which savings account will earn you the most money?
One that earns simple interest daily.
One that earns simple interest monthly.
One that compounds interest daily.
One that compounds interest monthly.
A savings account that compounds interest daily will earn you the most money.

Which of the following statements about savings accounts is FALSE?
Savings accounts limit the number of withdrawals that can be made each month.
Savings accounts are best used to store money for longer-term goals.
Savings accounts may require you to maintain a minimum balance to avoid paying a fee.
Savings accounts don't usually pay interest on the
Savings accounts don't usually pay interest on the FALSE statement about savings accounts. This statement is incorrect because savings accounts do usually pay interest on the deposited money.

Which of the following is NOT a common feature of a financial institution?
Access to ATMS
Access to investment products
Paper checks
Direct deposit

Paper checks are NOT a common feature of a financial institution. While some financial institutions may still offer paper checks, they are becoming less common as electronic payments become more prevalent.

How can you avoid spending more than what is in your bank account?
Check your bank statement once a month.
Ask your financial institution to notify you when you are close to $0 in your account.
Keep your own records to compare with your financial institution's records.
None of the above
Ask your financial institution to notify you when you are close to $0 in your account. This can help you avoid spending more than what is in your bank account.

Which of the following statements about check cashing companies is FALSE?
They offer bad deals where you'll owe a lot more than you borrowed.
They can take a percentage of your check on top of fees.
They make it easy to fall into a loan cycle that is hard to get out of.
They charge low fees.
They charge low fees is a FALSE statement about check cashing companies. In reality, check cashing companies often charge high fees that can take a large percentage of your check.

Which type of bank account typically offers the least (if any) interest?
Money market account
Savings account
Certificate of deposit
Checking account
Checking account typically offers the least interest (if any).

Which of the following statements about savings accounts is FALSE?
Savings accounts don't usually require a minimum balance.
Savings accounts pay interest on the money you deposit.
Savings accounts are best used to store money for longer-term goals.
Savings accounts limit the number of withdrawals that can be made each month.

Savings accounts are best used to store money for longer-term goals is a FALSE statement about savings accounts. Savings accounts are best used for storing money for short-to-medium-term goals rather than long-term goals, as they generally offer lower interest rates than other investment options such as stocks and bonds.

Which of the following statements is TRUE?
If there is a mistake on one of your bank accounts, there is nothing you can do about it.
If there is a mistake on one of your bank accounts, you should wait 30 days as these issues tend to resolve themselves.
If there is a mistake on your bank account, you should contact your financial institution immediately.
None of the above.
If there is a mistake on your bank account, you should contact your financial institution immediately.

Which of the following statements about investing is FALSE?
Investing is riskier than putting money in a savings account.
Investing is a guaranteed way to grow your money.
On average, investing money in the stock market earns a higher return than putting money in a savings account.
Investing is best for long-term financial goals, like paying for retirement.
Investing is a guaranteed way to grow your money is a FALSE statement about investing. Investment comes with risks and returns are not guaranteed.

What should you do before you withdraw money from the ATM?
Find a hidden ATM so no one will see you withdraw money.
Ask someone nearby for help using the ATM.
Inspect the ATM to make sure it wasn't tampered with.
Share your pin number with trusted friends so you don't forget it.
Inspect the ATM to make sure it wasn't tampered with. This is an important safety measure as scammers can place fake card readers or cameras on ATMs to steal your information.

How can you avoid spending more than what is in your bank account?
Check your bank statement once a month.
Ask your financial institution to notify you when you are close to $0 in your account.
Keep your own records to compare with your financial institution's records.
None of the above
Ask your financial institution to notify you when you are close to $0 in your account. This can help you avoid spending more than what is in your bank account.

Which of the following statements about check cashing companies is FALSE?
They can take a percentage of your check on top of fees.
They make it easy to fall into a loan cycle that is hard to get out of.
They offer bad deals where you'll owe a lot more than you borrowed.
They charge low fees.
They charge low fees is a FALSE statement about check cashing companies. In reality, check cashing companies often charge high fees that can take a large percentage of your check.

Why is it important to reconcile your bank statements?
To avoid spending more than what is in your account.
To detect any errors in your account.
To determine if you were charged any fees.
All of the above

To detect any errors in your account. This is the primary reason for reconciling your bank statements. It is important to make sure that all transactions in your account are correct, and to catch any errors or fraudulent activities in the account. The other options can also be important reasons for reconciling bank statements.

Savings accounts typically offer more interest than what type of account?
Money market account
Checking account
Retirement account
Certificate of deposit
Checking account typically offers lower interest rates (if any) compared to savings accounts.

If there is a mistake with one of your bank accounts, who should you contact to resolve the issue?
No one, you can't resolve the issue
The CFPB
Your financial institution
The local police station
Your financial institution is the right place to contact if there is a mistake with one of your bank accounts.

Which of the following statements about savings accounts is FALSE?
Savings accounts may require you to maintain a minimum balance to avoid paying a fee.
Savings accounts are best used to store money for longer-term goals.
Savings accounts pay interest on the money you deposit.
Savings accounts allow an unlimited amount of withdrawals each month.

Savings accounts allow an unlimited amount of withdrawals each month is a FALSE statement about savings accounts. In reality, savings accounts generally have a limit on the number of withdrawals that can be made per month due to federal regulations, usually up to six per month.

A savings account that compounds interest daily will earn a higher return than a savings account that pays simple interest daily.
TRUE
FALSE
TRUE. A savings account that compounds interest daily will earn a higher return than a savings account that pays simple interest daily.

Which of the following statements about check cashing companies is FALSE?
They make it easy to fall into a loan cycle that is hard to get out of.
They can take a percentage of your check on top of fees.
They offer bad deals where you'll owe a lot more than you borrowed.
They charge low fees.
They charge low fees is a FALSE statement about check cashing companies. In reality, check cashing companies often charge high fees that can take a large percentage of your check.

A stock is ________.
A type of savings account that pays interest based on current interest rates in the money market.
A type of investment that invests in a mix of different types of investments.
A share of ownership in a company.
A type of debt investment that acts like a loan.
A stock is a share of ownership in a company.

Question
8
/
10
Which of the following statements about investing is FALSE?
On average, investing money in the stock market earns a higher return than putting money in a savings account.
Investing is riskier than putting money in a savings account.
Investing is a guaranteed way to grow your money.
Investing is best for long-term financial goals, like paying for retirement.
Investing is a guaranteed way to grow your money is a FALSE statement about investing. Investments come with risks, and returns are not guaranteed.

What should you do before you approach an ATM?
Make sure it is hidden so no one will see you withdraw money.
Check for any suspicious people lurking nearby.
Share a picture of your debit card with your friends.
Ask someone nearby for help using the ATM.
Check for any suspicious people lurking nearby. This is an important safety measure as scammers may try to steal your information or money while you're approaching or leaving the ATM.

Which of the following is NOT a common feature of a financial institution?
Direct deposit
Paper checks
Access to ATMS
Access to investment products
Paper checks are becoming less common and are slowly being replaced by electronic payments. Therefore, paper checks are NOT a common feature of a financial institution.
Why is it important to follow up with customers both after a sale and even if the sale hasn’t been closed?

It is important to follow up with customers both after a sale and even if the sale hasn't been closed to build and maintain relationships with customers, and to enhance customer satisfaction. By following up with a customer after a sale, a business can show that they care about the customer's experience and are invested in their satisfaction. This can lead to repeat purchases, positive feedback to other potential customers, and a stronger reputation for providing good customer service. Following up with customers after unsuccessful sales attempts can also help to identify reasons for non-purchase and improve the sales process in the future. Additionally, even if the sale hasn’t been closed, following up with a prospective customer can help to maintain engagement, demonstrate continued effort and interest, and potentially lead to a future sale.
Why is it important to follow up with customers both after a sale and even if the sale hasn’t been closed?  short answer

It is important to follow up with customers both after a sale and even if the sale hasn’t been closed to enhance customer satisfaction, build and maintain relationships with customers, identify reasons for non-purchase and improve the sales process in the future, demonstrate continued effort and interest in potential customers, and potentially lead to future sales.
Follow-up is most important in what kinds of selling situations? short answer

Follow-up is most important in complex or high-value selling situations that involve a longer sales cycle and a higher level of investment from the customer. In these situations, following up can help build and maintain a strong relationship with the customer, address any concerns or objections they may have, and maintain engagement throughout the sales process. Additionally, following up after the sale can help to ensure that the customer is satisfied with their purchase and can help to identify opportunities for future sales or referrals.
Follow-up is most important in what kinds of selling situations? simple answer
Follow-up is most important in complex or high-value selling situations that require a longer sales cycle and a higher level of investment from the customer.
What benefits do salespeople receive from following up with customers? simple answer

Salespeople can benefit from following up with customers in several ways. By following up, they can build and maintain relationships with customers, enhance customer satisfaction, identify reasons for non-purchase and improve the sales process in the future, demonstrate continued effort and interest in potential customers, and potentially lead to future sales or referrals.

1. Making phone calls to customers

[ Choose ]
Choose what? Please provide more context or options to choose from.
Follow-up is essential for maintaining long-term buyer-seller relationships. Indicate whether the activity is a follow-up activity or a non-follow-up activity.

Making phone calls to customers can be a follow-up activity if it is done to keep in touch, address concerns or objections, or maintain engagement throughout the sales process. It can also be a non-follow-up activity if it is done for other purposes such as prospecting or setting appointments.

2. Providing proper service - Follow-up and non-follow-up activity
3. Advertising a new product - Non-follow-up activity

5. Meeting agreed-upon delivery schedules and arrangements - Follow-up activity

7. Obtaining spare parts - Non-follow-up activity

9. Billing properly - Non-follow-up activity
10. Creating Interest in a product - Non-follow-up activity
11. Identifying your target market - Non-follow-up activity
12. Providing needed manuals or handbooks - Follow-up and non-follow-up activity
13. Closing a sale - Can be a follow-up or non-follow-up activity
14. Sending simple questionnaires - Follow-up activity
15. Demonstrating a product - Can be a follow-up or non-follow-up activity
16. Connecting with customers through social media - Follow-up activity
17. Using suggesting - Non-follow-up activity
18. Securing credit arrangements - Non-follow-up activity
19. Assisting with warranty or service contracts - Follow-up activity
20. Determining distribution channels for a product - Non-follow-up activity
